papers.name: Paper-I: General Aptitude (common to all disciplines)

pattern.sectionalCutoffs: Per-paper qualifying marks (out of 100): UR/EWS 40, OBC-NCL 35, SC/ST/PwBD 30. Must qualify Paper-I AND Paper-II separately; final merit on combined 200.
pattern.qualifyingCriteria: Qualify each paper separately (per-paper floor), then merit-ranked on the combined 200-mark CBT total. Stages: CBT (only scored stage) → Document Verification → Initial Medical Examination (both pass/fail). No interview.

topics.name: Indian History, Polity & Geography
topics.syllabusText: Indian history, geography, constitution and polity. Foundational static GK commonly tested in PSU aptitude papers.

topics.name: Economy, General Science & Environment
topics.syllabusText: Indian economy, basic general science, and environment/ecology topics relevant to the coal sector.

topics.name: Current Affairs & Coal-Sector/PSU GK
topics.syllabusText: National and international current affairs, awards, sports, plus coal-sector and PSU-specific general knowledge.

topics.name: Verbal & Logical Reasoning
topics.syllabusText: Series, blood relations, coding-decoding, syllogisms, analogies, and statement-conclusion logic.

topics.name: Analytical & Spatial Reasoning
topics.syllabusText: Seating arrangement, puzzles, direction sense, spatial reasoning, and non-verbal pattern recognition.

topics.name: Data Interpretation
topics.syllabusText: Interpretation of tables, bar/line charts, and pie diagrams to draw quantitative conclusions.

topics.name: Arithmetic & Number Systems
topics.syllabusText: Number systems, averages, ratio-proportion, percentage, profit and loss, and simple/compound interest.

topics.name: Time-Distance-Work & Mensuration
topics.syllabusText: Time, speed and distance, time and work, and mensuration of 2D/3D figures.

topics.name: Probability, Statistics & DI
topics.syllabusText: Basic probability, statistics, and quantitative data interpretation.

topics.name: Vocabulary & Grammar
topics.syllabusText: Synonyms/antonyms, idioms and phrases, error spotting, and grammatical correctness. This section is presented in English only.

topics.name: Reading Comprehension & Sentence Construction
topics.syllabusText: Reading comprehension passages, sentence rearrangement/construction, and fill-in-the-blanks.

topics.name: C Programming & Recursion
topics.syllabusText: C programming fundamentals, control flow, pointers, functions, and recursion.

topics.name: Arrays, Stacks, Queues & Linked Lists
topics.syllabusText: Linear data structures: arrays, stacks, queues, and linked lists with their operations.

topics.name: Trees, Heaps, Hashing & Graphs
topics.syllabusText: Non-linear structures: binary trees, BST, heaps, hashing, and graph representations.

topics.name: Asymptotic Complexity & Analysis
topics.syllabusText: Time and space complexity, asymptotic notation, and recurrence analysis.

topics.name: Searching & Sorting
topics.syllabusText: Linear/binary search and comparison/non-comparison sorting algorithms with their complexities.

topics.name: Greedy, Divide-and-Conquer & Dynamic Programming
topics.syllabusText: Algorithm design paradigms: greedy methods, divide-and-conquer, and dynamic programming.

topics.name: Graph Algorithms
topics.syllabusText: Graph traversals (BFS/DFS), shortest paths, and minimum spanning trees.

topics.name: ER Model & Relational Algebra
topics.syllabusText: Entity-relationship modeling, the relational model, and relational algebra/calculus.

topics.name: SQL
topics.syllabusText: SQL queries, joins, aggregation, nested queries, and DDL/DML operations.

topics.name: Functional Dependencies & Normalization
topics.syllabusText: Functional dependencies and normal forms from 1NF through BCNF.

topics.name: Transactions, Concurrency & Indexing
topics.syllabusText: Transaction management, concurrency control, recovery, and indexing including B+ trees.

topics.name: Processes, Threads & CPU Scheduling
topics.syllabusText: Process and thread concepts, process states, and CPU scheduling algorithms.

topics.name: Synchronization & Deadlocks
topics.syllabusText: Process synchronization, semaphores, critical section problem, and deadlock handling.

topics.name: Memory Management & Virtual Memory
topics.syllabusText: Paging, segmentation, virtual memory, page replacement, and memory allocation.

topics.name: File Systems & Linux Administration
topics.syllabusText: File system organization, disk scheduling, and basic Linux/system administration (IT stream).

topics.name: OSI & TCP/IP Models
topics.syllabusText: Layered network architecture: OSI and TCP/IP reference models and their functions.

topics.name: IP Addressing, Subnetting & Routing
topics.syllabusText: IPv4/IPv6 addressing, subnetting, routing algorithms, and switching.

topics.name: Transport & Application Protocols
topics.syllabusText: Transport layer (TCP/UDP) and application protocols such as HTTP, DNS, and FTP.

topics.name: Data Communication & Network Security
topics.syllabusText: Data communication fundamentals, encoding/multiplexing, and network security basics.

topics.name: Number Representation & ALU
topics.syllabusText: Machine number representation, fixed/floating point, and ALU operations.

topics.name: Instruction Formats, Datapath & Control
topics.syllabusText: Instruction formats, addressing modes, CPU datapath, and control unit design.

topics.name: Pipelining, Memory Hierarchy & I/O
topics.syllabusText: Pipelining and hazards, memory hierarchy, cache, and I/O with interrupts.

topics.name: Boolean Algebra & Combinational Circuits
topics.syllabusText: Boolean algebra, logic gates, K-map minimization, and combinational circuits (mux, decoder, adder).

topics.name: Sequential Circuits
topics.syllabusText: Flip-flops, registers, counters, and synchronous/asynchronous sequential circuit design.

topics.name: Finite Automata & Regular Languages
topics.syllabusText: DFA/NFA, regular expressions, regular languages, and the pumping lemma.

topics.name: CFG, Turing Machines & Decidability
topics.syllabusText: Context-free grammars and PDA, Turing machines, and decidability/undecidability.

topics.name: Lexical Analysis & Parsing
topics.syllabusText: Lexical analysis and parsing techniques including LL and LR parsing.

topics.name: Syntax-Directed Translation & Code Optimization
topics.syllabusText: Syntax-directed translation, intermediate code generation, runtime environments, and code optimization.

topics.name: SDLC & System Analysis
topics.syllabusText: Software development life cycle models, system analysis, and design. Lower-confidence portal-inferred IT-stream topic.

topics.name: Web Technologies & Internet Fundamentals
topics.syllabusText: HTML/CSS/JavaScript, client-server architecture, and internet fundamentals. Lower-confidence portal-inferred IT-stream topic.

meta.notes: Pattern (2 papers x 100 Q, 1 mark/Q, 200 total, 180 min single timer, NO negative marking, bilingual EN/HI except General English, per-paper cutoffs 40/35/30) confirmed against official CIL Advt. No. 03/2026. Paper-II carries only published subject NAMES; topic-level detail is mapped to the GATE CS syllabus (sources recommend GATE as prep reference). Paper-I 25/25/25/25 section split and per-topic importance are portal-inferred (lower confidence). 'software-engineering-web' is a low-confidence IT-stream extra. Verify Paper-II weighting before high-stakes mock weighting (M5).
